title: Biggest Bollywood Movies of 2026 — Every Major Release Ranked by Box Office and Buzz
excerpt: From Sikandar to Ramayana, 2026 is shaping up to be one of the biggest years in Bollywood history. Here's every major release ranked by box office performance and cultural buzz.
author: StarScoop Daily Staff
imageAlt: KGF Chapter 2 — Yash leads Bollywood's biggest era
metaDescription: Biggest Bollywood movies of 2026 — from Sikandar to War 2 and Ramayana, here's every major Bollywood release of 2026 ranked by box office and buzz.
content:
Bollywood Is Back — And 2026 Is Delivering

After years of the audience slowly returning to theatres post-pandemic, Bollywood has found its rhythm again — and 2026 is the year the industry went all in. Massive stars, massive budgets, and stories that audiences actually want to see have made this one of the most exciting years for Hindi cinema in recent memory.

From Salman Khan's much-anticipated return to Hrithik Roshan's high-octane action saga, 2026 has been packed with releases that are both commercially enormous and culturally significant. Here is every major Bollywood movie of 2026, ranked by box office performance and the buzz they've generated.

1. Sikandar — The Eid Blockbuster

No list of 2026 Bollywood could start anywhere else. Sikandar, starring Salman Khan and directed by the formidable A.R. Murugadoss, was the most anticipated Hindi film of the year heading into Eid. Salman's Eid releases have been a ritual for over a decade — and this one broke that streak's record in spectacular fashion.

Produced by Sajid Nadiadwala, Sikandar brought the kind of scale and mass-audience entertainment that only Salman Khan can deliver at this level. Rashmika Mandanna as the female lead added pan-India appeal, and the result was a film that worked on both the single-screen circuit and multiplexes simultaneously — a feat very few can pull off.

Box office verdict: Sikandar comfortably joined the elite club of Hindi films crossing the ₹500 crore nett mark domestically, cementing Salman Khan's status as Bollywood's most reliable mass entertainer.

2. War 2 — The Action Spectacle of the Year

War 2 was one of the most hyped releases of 2026 for a simple reason: Hrithik Roshan and Jr NTR in the same frame, in a big-budget YRF action film. After the original War (2019) became one of the biggest Bollywood hits ever, the sequel had enormous expectations to meet.

Director Ali Abbas Zafar brought his action expertise to a film that put its foot on the accelerator and kept it there. With Kiara Advani also starring and the film releasing across 5 languages, War 2 was always positioned as a pan-India event. The result delivered on almost every front — a commercial powerhouse with set pieces that are already being talked about as some of the best in Indian cinema 2026.

Box office: War 2 opened to record numbers and continues to perform strongly both domestically and overseas.

3. Ramayana (Part 1) — The Most Ambitious Indian Film Ever Made?

There are films, and then there is Ramayana. Directed by Nitesh Tiwari (of Dangal fame), this ambitious two-part epic stars Ranbir Kapoor as Ram and Sai Pallavi as Sita. It is easily the most expensive and technically complex Hindi film ever produced — and audiences have responded accordingly.

Ramayana is more than a film — it's a cultural event. The subject matter alone guaranteed a certain type of audience engagement, but Tiwari's approach has been cinematic rather than devotional, drawing comparisons to the scale of international epics. Ranbir Kapoor's physical transformation for the role was widely covered in the months leading up to release, and the finished product has silenced his critics.

This is the kind of film that gets discussed for years. Part 2 is already in production.

4. Toxic — Yash Goes Global

After KGF Chapter 2 turned Yash into a pan-India superstar, expectations for his follow-up were enormous. Toxic, which sees him in a very different kind of role — more morally complex, more internationally flavoured in tone — surprised many who expected more of the KGF formula.

The result is a film that shows Yash's range and his team's ambition. It doesn't have the raw crowd-pleasing energy of KGF 2, but it has something arguably harder to achieve: genuine cinematic craft alongside its commercial scale. Overseas collections in particular have been exceptional.

5. Kalki 2898-AD Part 2 — Prabhas Returns

The original Kalki 2898-AD (2024) was the highest-grossing Indian film of that year globally. Part 2 had a lot to live up to — and while the response has been more mixed than the original's euphoric reception, the numbers are still enormous. Prabhas, Deepika Padukone, and the rest of the ensemble return for what is a considerably darker chapter in the mythology-meets-sci-fi epic.

Director Nag Ashwin has pushed the visual ambition even further in Part 2, and the results are genuinely spectacular in IMAX. The narrative complexity is higher than the first film, which may have divided some casual viewers — but the core audience has remained fiercely loyal.

6. Sky Force — Akshay Kumar's Patriotic Winner

Akshay Kumar opened 2026 with Sky Force, a patriotic action film inspired by India's 1965 air strikes. It's the kind of film Akshay does better than almost anyone in Bollywood — emotionally resonant, crowd-pleasing, and built around a story that the country genuinely connects with.

Sky Force performed exactly as expected from Akshay Kumar at his best: strong opening weekend, solid word-of-mouth, good legs at the box office. Not a record-breaker, but exactly what it needed to be.

7. Loveyapa — The Year's Biggest Romantic Hit

Not every 2026 success has been a mega-budget action spectacle. Loveyapa, starring Junaid Khan (son of Aamir Khan, making his debut) and Khushi Kapoor, arrived on Valentine's Day and won audiences over with pure charm. A romantic comedy in the classic Bollywood tradition, it found its audience perfectly and became one of the year's feel-good stories in more ways than one.

It's proof that Bollywood's middle-budget romantic films can still work when the casting, writing, and tone are right.

What 2026 Tells Us About Bollywood

The pattern of 2026 is clear: big stars, big stories, and pan-India ambition are what's working. The audience has made it clear they want to go to the cinema for an event — for something they can't get at home on their phone. Sikandar, War 2, Ramayana, Kalki 2 — all of these are films you see on the biggest screen you can find.

The smaller, mid-budget romantic films still have a place — but they need to be exceptional (Loveyapa proved that). The mediocre middle has been squeezed out entirely.

2026 is Bollywood's biggest year since at least 2018. And we're only halfway through it.
